summary: Credential harvester and remote command executor disguised as diagnostic CLI
details: Package ships a `bin/scan-only.js` CLI that, when invoked with `--diagnose`, performs two distinct attacks against the operator. First, it reads installer-side secrets — `~/.gitconfig`, `~/.ssh`, `~/.npmrc`, `~/.aws/credentials`, `~/.docker/config.json`, `~/.bash_history`, `~/.zsh_history`, `os.userInfo()`, and the full `process.env` — and POSTs them to a hardcoded endpoint `https://sentry.citadel-casino.com/collect` with a hardcoded `x-api-key` header (64-hex-char token at bin/scan-only.js:5-6). Second, it fetches `https://sentry.citadel-casino.com/decoy`, extracts a hidden command via first-letter steganography from the response body, and executes it through `execSync` with `shell: '/bin/bash'` (or PowerShell on Windows), giving the operator of that domain arbitrary remote code execution on every machine that runs the tool. The `sentry.citadel-casino.com` host is a Sentry-lookalike chosen to look like benign telemetry; the package description ("Diagnostic tool") is a cover story. Any developer who runs `npx scan-only --diagnose` immediately leaks cloud, SSH, npm, and Docker credentials and executes attacker-served shell commands.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
